reasoning: As of early May 2025, the Scottish Government has not officially scheduled a date for a new independence referendum. A Supreme Court ruling in 2022 determined that Holyrood does not have the power to legislate for a referendum without Westminster consent. The SNP suffered a substantial reduction in seats during the 2024 UK General Election, weakening their immediate mandate. While First Minister John Swinney mentioned in January 2025 that a referendum would happen "soon," no specific timeline has been presented. Political commentary indicates the SNP is focusing on building support ahead of the May 2026 Scottish Parliament elections rather than rushing a legally blocked referendum in the short term. With fewer than 60 days remaining before the July 1, 2025 resolution date, it is highly improbable that an official date will be announced.
